headline: Israel Declares Indefinite IDF Presence as US‑Iran Deal Stalls in Levant
subheadline: Defense Minister Katz said IDF will hold security zones in Lebanon, Syria and Gaza indefinitely, clearing residents and terror infrastructure, while the US‑Iran digital pact yields no asset releases.
lede: Israel's defence minister announced that the IDF will maintain an indefinite presence in security zones across southern Lebanon, Syria and Gaza, ordering the removal of local residents and destruction of terror infrastructure. The United States and Iran signed a brief digital agreement, yet US officials confirmed that no Iranian frozen assets have been unfrozen and sanctions remain in place. Iran meanwhile plans to impose service fees on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz after a 60‑day window, adding another flashpoint to regional tensions.

sections.title: Shahed Drone Strikes Intensify Kyiv Threat
sections.body: A Russian Shahed‑2 drone hit Kyiv's historic Pechersk Lavra, causing damage and prompting Ukrainian officials to blame a Russian UAV rather than a Patriot missile. Multiple Shahed attacks were also reported in Mykolaiv, Kirovohrad, Dnipropetrovsk and Sumy, targeting civilian infrastructure. Lockheed Martin warned it cannot guarantee Patriot missile deliveries to Ukraine despite a $4.7 billion production deal. Finland warned Ukraine that its drones must not endanger NATO member security.

sections.title: Israel Extends Security Zones Across Fronts
sections.body: Israel's defence minister Katz declared that IDF forces will remain in security zones in Lebanon, Syria and Gaza without a time limit, clearing local residents and destroying terror infrastructure, including houses in contact‑line villages. He warned that any Iranian attack on Israel stemming from Lebanon would be met with full‑force Israeli strikes. The United States and Iran signed a brief digital agreement, but US Vice‑President Vance confirmed no Iranian assets have been released and sanctions stay in effect. Iran plans to levy service fees on ships passing the Strait of Hormuz after a 60‑day grace period.

sections.title: Intelligence Assessment
sections.body: The indefinite IDF presence sustains a high‑risk environment that could trigger Hezbollah or Iranian retaliation, while the stalled US‑Iran agreement leaves economic pressure on Tehran unchanged. Concurrently, persistent Shahed drone attacks underscore Ukraine's acute low‑altitude air‑defence challenges, and diplomatic setbacks may embolden Russian claims.
